quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 2 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: Advantages, Disadvantages, and Applications of Friction and Forces
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question:
Based on the technician's inspection, which of the following is identified as a primary disadvantage of friction in this system?

"An industrial technician is inspecting a high-speed conveyor belt system that has been operating continuously for several weeks. He notices that the rubber surface of the rollers has become significantly thinner and more brittle than when they were first installed. Furthermore, he records that the metal bearings surrounding the central axle are radiating intense heat, requiring a constant flow of coolant to prevent the machinery from seizing. The technician concludes that a large portion of the electrical energy supplied to the motor is being converted into unwanted thermal energy and physical debris rather than useful motion."
options: It increases the electrical conductivity of the motor
It results in the gradual wearing away of material surfaces
It causes the conveyor belt to move at unpredictable speeds
It eliminates the need for external cooling systems
questions.explanation: The passage describes rubber surfaces becoming thinner and more brittle, and energy being converted into unwanted thermal energy and debris. This shows friction causing wear and tear.

questions.question:
Based on the safety engineer's report, what is the primary advantage of friction in this scenario?

"A safety engineer is evaluating a new flooring material designed for use in hospital emergency rooms. During the test, a robotic walker is pushed across a surface coated with a thin layer of liquid. On the original tile, the walker's wheels spin rapidly without moving the frame forward, causing it to veer off course. However, when the new textured material is installed, the wheels 'grab' the surface effectively, allowing the walker to come to a precise stop even on the wet floor. The engineer notes that without this specific resistance, the transition of energy from the motor to the floor would be impossible, leading to a total loss of directional control."
options: It reduces the amount of power the motor needs to operate
It prevents the liquid from spreading across the floor
It enables the wheels to exert the force necessary for motion
It increases the total weight of the robotic walker for stability
questions.explanation: Without friction, the wheels spin in place and cannot transfer force to the floor. Friction enables the wheels to grip and generate forward motion.

questions.question:
Based on the laws of physics and the description in the passage, what is the error in the student's Free-Body Diagram?

"A student is drawing a Free-Body Diagram (FBD) to represent a heavy crate being pushed at a constant velocity across a rough horizontal floor. He draws a central box to represent the crate and adds four primary vectors. He includes a downward arrow labeled 'Gravity' ($F_g$) and an upward arrow of equal length labeled 'Normal Force' ($F_N$). He then adds a long arrow pointing to the right to represent the 'Applied Force' ($F_{app}$) from the person pushing. Finally, he adds a shorter arrow pointing to the left to represent 'Kinetic Friction' ($F_k$)."
options: The Normal Force should be longer than the Gravity vector
The Kinetic Friction vector should be equal in length to the Applied Force vector
The Applied Force vector should be pointing in the same direction as Friction
The diagram should not include a Normal Force because the crate is moving
questions.explanation: At constant velocity, net force is zero. The applied force and kinetic friction must be equal in magnitude, so their arrows should be the same length.

questions.question:
Based on the scientist's findings, what can be inferred about the coefficient of friction (μ) between the aluminum and the two surfaces?

"A materials scientist is testing how different coatings affect the movement of an aluminum block. In the first trial, the block is placed on a sheet of polished glass; she observes that it takes only 4 N of force to keep the block sliding at a steady pace. In the second trial, she replaces the glass with a sheet of high-grip industrial rubber. Using the exact same aluminum block, she finds that it now requires 22 N of force to maintain the same steady speed. The scientist concludes that while the weight of the block and the normal force remained identical in both trials, the fundamental 'roughness' of the interface had changed."
options: It is a fixed value that depends only on the aluminum block's mass
It is directly related to the coarseness of a surface
It decreased in the second trial because the rubber provided more support
It is determined by the speed at which the block moves across the sheets
questions.explanation: The coefficient of friction depends on the materials in contact. Rougher surfaces (rubber vs. polished glass) have a higher coefficient of friction.

questions.question:
Based on the curator's actions, what occurred when the second cable was added to the system?

"An art gallery curator is hanging a heavy bronze sculpture from the ceiling using a single vertical steel cable. He notices that the cable appears extremely taut and is concerned about it snapping. To distribute the load, he adds a second identical cable, attaching it to the same hook on the sculpture but anchoring it to a different point on the ceiling, so the two cables now form a 'V' shape. Although the sculpture's mass hasn't changed, the curator observes that the original cable is no longer as strained, as the two lines now share the burden of the sculpture's pull toward the floor."
options: The total gravitational force acting on the sculpture was reduced
The tension in the original cable decreased as the load was shared
The sculpture's weight was doubled by the addition of the second cable
The cables exerted a horizontal friction force to keep the sculpture still
questions.explanation: Adding a second cable distributes the load. The total weight is unchanged, but each cable now supports a portion, reducing the tension in the original cable.

questions.question:
Based on the engineer's specifications, why was it necessary to increase the friction on the highway?

"A civil engineer is reviewing the safety specifications for a mountain highway frequently subjected to heavy rainfall. She notes that the original asphalt becomes dangerously slick when wet, causing vehicle tires to lose their grip and skid during turns. To address this, she orders the application of a coarse-aggregate overlay—a layer of crushed stone mixed with a high-friction resin. The engineer explains that by increasing the tactile irregularities of the road surface, the tires can maintain a reliable hold even in the presence of a lubricating water film, effectively preventing the vehicles from sliding off the embankment."
options: To reduce the wear and tear on vehicle brake pads
To allow the road to absorb more water during heavy rainfall
To ensure the tires could generate enough force to maintain a path
To decrease the engine power required to climb the mountain
questions.explanation: Higher friction allows tires to grip the road and maintain directional control during turns, preventing skidding off the embankment.

questions.question:
According to the supervisor's investigation, what is the primary result of the low friction caused by the oil?

"A factory supervisor is investigating why a mechanical sorting arm is failing to move glass bottles along a conveyor belt. He discovers that a nearby machine has leaked a thin film of industrial oil onto the belt's surface. Although the belt is spinning at its usual speed, the bottles remain in place, with the belt merely sliding beneath them. The supervisor notes that the oil has filled the microscopic gaps between the glass and the rubber, creating a nearly seamless barrier that prevents the belt from 'locking' with the base of the bottles to push them forward."
options: The mass of the glass bottles has significantly increased
The conveyor belt is unable to transfer its motion to the bottles
The internal motor of the belt has lost its electrical power
The bottles have become physically fused to the conveyor belt
questions.explanation: The oil reduces friction so much that the belt cannot grip the bottles. Motion cannot be transferred from the belt to the bottles.

questions.question:
Based on the instructor's explanation of the third law of motion, which statement describes the interaction between the diver and the water?

"A scuba diver is practicing propulsion techniques in a deep-water training tank. To move forward, she wears large fins and performs a powerful kick, pushing a mass of water backward away from her body. The diver observes that as her fins displace the water in one direction, her entire body is propelled in the exact opposite direction. Her instructor explains that the force she exerts on the water is perfectly matched by the force the water exerts on her fins, confirming that it is actually the water's 'push back' that facilitates her movement through the tank."
options: The diver must push harder than the water pushes back to move forward
The force from the water on the fins occurs after the diver's kick is complete
The forces exerted by the diver and the water are equal in magnitude
The water's resistance is a type of friction that prevents the diver from moving
questions.explanation: Newton's third law states that forces come in equal and opposite pairs. The force the diver exerts on the water equals the force the water exerts on the diver.

questions.question:
Based on the passage, how does the pry bar affect the application of the carpenter's force?

"A carpenter is using a pry bar to remove a stubborn nail from a wooden plank. He slides the flat end of the bar under the head of the nail and applies a sharp, downward force to the opposite end of the handle. This motion causes the pry bar to pivot against the wood, transforming his downward push into a powerful upward pull on the nail. The carpenter notes that without the rigid structure of the metal bar to redirect his effort, he would not be able to generate the specific vertical lift required to overcome the wood's grip on the nail."
options: It breaks the forcefield around the mass with an even greater force
It changes the direction of the applied force to pull it up
It reduces the friction between the nail and the wooden plank
It allows the carpenter to apply a force without touching the tool
questions.explanation: The pry bar acts as a lever, changing the direction of the applied force. A downward push on the handle becomes an upward pull on the nail.

questions.question:
Based on the specifications provided in the passage, what is the magnitude of the force the motor must apply to achieve the target acceleration?

"A laboratory drone is designed to transport medical samples between stations. During a calibration test, the drone, which has a total mass of 800 g, is programmed to accelerate from rest to a specific velocity along a horizontal guide rail. The onboard computer monitors the electric motor as it provides a steady horizontal thrust. To achieve the required test acceleration of 4.5 m/s², the computer must calculate the exact amount of force needed, assuming that friction from the guide rail has been neutralized by magnetic levitation."

Based on the researcher's observations, what physics principle explains why the mannequin continued to move forward after the car stopped?

"A crash-test researcher is observing the behavior of a 75 kg mannequin seated in a vehicle traveling at 22 m/s. The vehicle is engineered to come to a sudden, complete stop upon impacting a specialized foam barrier. During the collision, the car decelerates to 0 m/s in less than half a second. However, the researcher notes that the mannequin continues to move forward at nearly the original speed of 22 m/s until it is intercepted by the tension of the seatbelt. The researcher explains that no new force was applied to push the mannequin forward; rather, the mannequin was simply 'attempting' to maintain its current state of motion."
options: An unbalanced forward force was generated by the car's engine during the crash
The mannequin possessed inertia, causing it to resist the change in its velocity
The foam barrier transferred its kinetic energy directly into the mannequin
The friction between the seat and the mannequin increased its forward momentum
questions.explanation: Inertia is the tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion. The mannequin continued moving forward because no force had yet acted on it to change its velocity.

questions.question:
Based on the diagnostic report, what occurred when the robotic explorer was moved from Earth to the lunar outpost?

"A robotic explorer is calibrated on Earth, where its high-precision internal sensors record a mass of 120 kg and a corresponding weight of 1,176 N. The explorer is then transported to a lunar outpost where the gravitational pull is approximately one-sixth as strong as Earth's. Upon arrival, the robot's diagnostic system runs a self-check. The sensors indicate that while the robot's structural frame and internal components remain entirely intact and unchanged, the downward force required to lift its own limbs has decreased significantly. The mission commander notes that the robot has not 'lost' any of its physical matter, yet it would now register a much lower value on a standard spring scale."
options: The robot's mass decreased because there is no atmosphere on the Moon
The gravitational constant of the Moon increased the robot's total matter
The robot's weight changed due to the weaker gravitational field
The internal sensors failed because mass and weight must always be equal
questions.explanation: Mass remains constant regardless of location. Weight (W = mg) depends on gravitational field strength, so the robot weighs less on the Moon.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered real-world applications of friction, forces, and Newton's laws!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the distinction between mass and weight, and how friction enables motion.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on free-body diagrams and the advantages vs. disadvantages of friction.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: Newton's laws, types of friction, and the difference between mass and weight.
